title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs mixer initialization, channels, latency, volume, mute, and cue alternatives from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded accessible audio and cue system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.
Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Mixer Init · changed case · Review Mixer Init
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 3723 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 6863 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A game without audio output should refuse to start.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics. Artifact under review: bounded accessible audio and cue system.

options.text: The Audio Failure record says bounded accessible audio and cue system looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Audio Failure.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Audio Failure for bounded accessible audio and cue system.
The record names the assumption, applies Audio Failure, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.why: Retrieving Audio Failure through the concrete bounded accessible audio and cue system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.
